report.executive_summary:
MEMORANDUM TO FILE - TITLE COMMITMENT ANALYSIS AND LEGAL REVIEW

RE: Title Commitment No. 01-26006219-01T
Property: 675 Bunker Hill Road, Columbia, MS 39429-7832 (Marion County, MS)
Parcel No.: 074-04-000-04-005
Proposed Buyer: Kyla B. Fortenberry
Proposed Lender: Society Mortgage
Underwriter: Old Republic National Title Insurance Company
Commitment Date: January 16, 2026
Current Readiness Score: 67/100

CRITICAL FINDING: This transaction is NOT READY FOR CLOSING. The title commitment contains three material defects that must be resolved before policy issuance can proceed. Most critically, Requirement 4 has not been satisfied due to a recorded deed conveying the property to incorrect grantees (Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson) rather than to the proposed buyer (Kyla B. Fortenberry). This creates a chain of title break that is fatal to the transaction. Additionally, a fundamental discrepancy exists between the vested owner (GARNER TREE FARM, LLC, a Mississippi limited liability company) and the seller of record (Terri G. Crawley, individually), which raises questions regarding authority to convey and member consent. Finally, required LLC organizational documentation has not been submitted for underwriter review. These issues must be resolved immediately to prevent transaction failure and potential liability exposure.

report.requirements:
SCHEDULE B, PART I - REQUIREMENTS STATUS

REQUIREMENT 1: Vested Owner vs. Seller Discrepancy
Description: Vested Owner is GARNER TREE FARM, LLC; Seller is TERRI G. CRAWLEY (individually). Advise customer service of circumstances explaining discrepancy.
Status: OPEN - NOT SATISFIED
Analysis: This requirement addresses a fundamental title defect. The commitment shows GARNER TREE FARM, LLC as the vested owner of record, yet the transaction documents identify Terri G. Crawley as the individual seller. This creates ambiguity regarding:
  (a) Whether Terri G. Crawley has authority to convey LLC property;
  (b) Whether the LLC has authorized this transaction;
  (c) Whether all four LLC members consent to the sale;
  (d) The proper chain of title and conveyancing authority.
Legal Implication: Without clarification and proper LLC authorization documentation, the title company cannot issue a policy insuring clear title to the buyer. This is a material defect requiring immediate resolution.

REQUIREMENT 2: Verify Open Deed of Trust
Description: No open deed of trust found in Marion County records. Verify by inquiry of escrow personnel and/or proposed insured.
Status: OPEN - PENDING VERIFICATION
Analysis: The commitment indicates no recorded deed of trust exists in Marion County records as of the commitment date. This requirement mandates verification that no other liens or encumbrances exist that would cloud title. Escrow personnel and the proposed buyer must confirm no other financing or security interests exist.

REQUIREMENT 3: Bankruptcy Search
Description: No bankruptcy proceedings found for GARNER TREE FARM, LLC, TERRI G. CRAWLEY, and KYLA B. FORTENBERRY.
Status: SATISFIED
Analysis: Bankruptcy searches have been completed for all relevant parties with negative results. No bankruptcy filings exist that would affect title or the transaction.

REQUIREMENT 4: Record Deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY [CRITICAL DEFECT]
Description: Record satisfactory executed Deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC, grantor(s) to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY, grantee(s), conveying the caption premises in Marion County records.
Status: OPEN - NOT SATISFIED - CRITICAL DEFECT
Analysis: THIS IS THE MOST CRITICAL ISSUE IN THIS TRANSACTION. The requirement mandates recording a deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC (the vested owner) to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY (the proposed buyer). However, the deed recorded on January 13, 2026 (Instrument #20260174) conveys the property to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson, NOT to Kyla B. Fortenberry.

This represents a complete failure to satisfy Requirement 4 and creates a fatal chain of title defect. The recorded deed names wrong grantees, which means:
  (a) Title has not passed to the intended buyer (Kyla B. Fortenberry);
  (b) The title company cannot issue an owner's policy to Kyla B. Fortenberry;
  (c) The buyer does not have legal ownership of the property;
  (d) The transaction cannot close in its current form.

Legal Requirement: A corrected deed must be executed by GARNER TREE FARM, LLC (or its authorized representatives) and recorded in Marion County records, naming KYLA B. FORTENBERRY as the sole grantee. This corrected deed must be recorded before the title commitment can be satisfied and before policy issuance can occur.

REQUIREMENT 5: Record Deed of Trust
Description: Record satisfactory executed Deed of Trust from KYLA B. FORTENBERRY as Trustor, in favor of SOCIETY MORTGAGE, as Beneficiary, securing an original principal balance of $80,808.00.
Status: OPEN - PENDING RECORDING
Analysis: The deed of trust securing the lender's loan must be recorded in Marion County records. This requirement cannot be satisfied until Requirement 4 is resolved (i.e., until title passes to Kyla B. Fortenberry). Once the corrected deed is recorded, the deed of trust must be executed and recorded to secure the lender's interest.

REQUIREMENT 6: LLC Documentation and Compliance [HIGH PRIORITY]
Description: Submit: (a) Copy of operating agreement and amendments or certification of none; (b) Evidence LLC is in good standing; (c) Resolution signed by all members authorizing transaction; (d) Other requirements following review of organizational documents.
Status: OPEN - NOT SATISFIED
Analysis: This requirement is essential to resolve the vested owner vs. seller discrepancy (Requirement 1). The title company must receive and review:

  (a) Operating Agreement: A certified copy of the LLC's operating agreement and any amendments, or a certification that no amendments exist. This document establishes the LLC's governance structure, member rights, and procedures for authorizing transactions.

  (b) Good Standing Certificate: Evidence that GARNER TREE FARM, LLC is in good standing with the Mississippi Secretary of State. This confirms the LLC is validly organized and authorized to conduct business.

  (c) Member Authorization Resolution: A resolution signed by ALL FOUR members (Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, and Karen Garner Delancey) authorizing the sale of the property to Kyla B. Fortenberry. This is critical because:
    - The operating agreement may require unanimous consent for asset sales;
    - Each member has a fiduciary duty to the LLC;
    - Without documented member consent, the transaction may be voidable;
    - The title company cannot insure against claims by non-consenting members.

  (d) Additional Requirements: Following underwriter review of the organizational documents, additional requirements may be imposed (e.g., certified member list, tax ID verification, etc.).

Legal Implication: Without these documents, the title company cannot confirm that the LLC has authority to convey the property or that all members have consented to the sale. This creates significant liability exposure for both the title company and the buyer.
